page: Amazon Web Services m5dn.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The system provides 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of 3600 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core CPU and memory performance. The instance achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 81302.2 and a PassMark CPU score of 55532.59, placing both in the top 25% of tested hardware. Memory read bandwidth is also highly rated, reaching 4502.91 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 215.51 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In application benchmarks, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in Redis SET operations, processing 74.23 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 44.21 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are average to poor, with the 7B model text generation registering at 6.1 tokens per second.

From a resource density perspective, the m5dn.24xlarge offers a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 1:4 alongside high-speed local NVMe storage and high-bandwidth networking. This hardware profile makes it highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring high network throughput and fast local scratch space without the premium of dedicated memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The server is well-suited for high-throughput databases, Redis caching layers, static web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by a dedicated 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance features 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong multi-core performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 55532.59, and high memory read bandwidth. It excels in high-throughput operations, achieving 74.23 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 44.21 GB/sec in static web serving. However, single-core and LLM text generation performance remain average to poor. This instance is highly suited for network-intensive workloads, high-throughput databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded enterprise applications requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.24xlarge is a 96-vCPU general-purpose instance with 384 GB RAM, 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth general-purpose platform combining ninety-six vCPUs, fast local NVMe storage, and one hundred gigabit network performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL processors
3600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput applications
Strong multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth performance
Optimized for Redis caching, static web serving, and high-throughput databases
